title: Subconvexity for L-functions on U(n) x U(n+1)
speaker: Simon Marshall
abstract: We prove this bound by first using the unitary Ichino-Ikeda formula of N. Harris to relate the central L-value to an automorphic period integral.  There is a `trivial' bound for this integral, which turns out to correspond to the convexity bound for the L-value if the test vector is chosen correctly.  We are able to improve the bound for the period integral using a technique called arithmetic amplification, which uses the action of the Hecke operators, and this yields a subconvex bound.

proof_stages.label: Ichino-Ikeda period reduction
proof_stages.summary: The subconvex problem is framed by using the Ichino-Ikeda formula to convert the central Rankin-Selberg L-value for a Gan-Gross-Prasad pair into a nonzero automorphic period.

proof_stages.label: P-adic-depth family with generic conductor
proof_stages.summary: The representations of U(n+1) x U(n) are restricted by bounded level away from a split finite place w, fixed definite archimedean data, temperedness, and principal-series local factors at w with generic character ratios ensuring maximal conductor and avoiding conductor drop.

proof_stages.label: Microlocal types and compatible vectors
proof_stages.summary: At the split place the speaker builds thickened torus types and chooses microlocalized test vectors transforming by the corresponding characters, with a compatibility condition on the intersection ensuring the period is not forced to vanish.

proof_stages.label: Convexity-normalized period bounds
proof_stages.summary: Classical and higher-dimensional period examples are used to identify when a trivial harmonic-analysis bound for a period has exactly the size of the convexity bound for the associated L-function.

proof_stages.label: Hecke amplification framework
proof_stages.summary: The Iwaniec-Sarnak pretrace-formula amplification is recalled: a Hecke operator is chosen to have a large eigenvalue on the target form, producing a strengthened spectral term and a geometric side involving controlled Hecke translates.

proof_stages.label: Relative off-diagonal control by microlocal transversality
proof_stages.summary: For relative periods the off-diagonal geometric terms become integrals pairing translated H-eigenfunctions, and microlocalized finite-place vectors make their phase-space/spatial concentration explicit enough for generic transversality to give a saving.

proof_stages.label: Power-saving period theorem
proof_stages.summary: For L2-normalized compatible microlocal vectors in the family, the H-period is bounded by a factor q^{(2-delta)l}, improving the trivial q^{2l}-sized bound by a power.

proof_stages.label: Subconvex L-value consequence
proof_stages.summary: After imposing local distinction and the hypotheses needed for Ichino-Ikeda, the period theorem yields a subconvex estimate L(1/2, BC(pi) x BC(pi_H)) bounded by the analytic conductor to 1/4 minus a positive delta.
